Their credentials go directly to <b data-path-to-node="7,0,0" data-index-in-node="80">Cognito</b>.<div>Upon successful authentication, Cognito issues a <b data-path-to-node="7,0,1,0,0" data-index-in-node="49">JWT (JSON Web Token)</b>. This token is stored int browser and attached to the header of every subsequent API requests.&nbsp;</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="370" x="1230" y="330" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="Ai0bs3T9j8OwdKe--Ouz-21" parent="1" style="rhombus;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;curveFitting=1;jiggle=2;fontStyle=1;fontFamily=Tahoma;fillColor=#e3c800;strokeColor=#B09500;fontColor=#000000;" value="<h2>5</h2>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="40" x="1180" y="418" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="Ai0bs3T9j8OwdKe--Ouz-22" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;fontSize=14;fontStyle=0;fontFamily=Tahoma;" value="<p data-path-to-node="9,0,0">When the user performs an action (e.g., <code data-path-to-node="9,0,0" data-index-in-node="77">POST /create-todo</code>), the request hits the <b data-path-to-node="9,0,0" data-index-in-node="118">API Gateway</b>.&nbsp;<span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">The Gateway automatically validates the JWT. If the token is missing or invalid, the request is rejected immediately at the door, saving you from unnecessary Lambda execution costs.</span></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="370" x="1230" y="428" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="Ai0bs3T9j8OwdKe--Ouz-23" parent="1" authorized, the request triggers a <b data-path-to-node="11,0,0" data-index-in-node="64">Lambda function</b>.</p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="370" x="1230" y="519" as="geometry" /> or unauthorized</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="50" width="370" x="1230" y="760" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="RduPqYsKie_LVsf-Kyjq-10" parent="1" id="RduPqYsKie_LVsf-Kyjq-14" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;fontSize=14;fontFamily=Tahoma;" value="EventBridge sends a real-time alert via <b data-path-to-node="10,2,0" data-index-in-node="66">SNS</b> to notify the security team via email or SMS." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="50" width="370" x="1230" y="920" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="RduPqYsKie_LVsf-Kyjq-15" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;fontSize=14;fontFamily=Tahoma;" value="This is the most advanced part of the flow. A dedicated <b>Lambda</b> function is automatically triggered to take immediate action, such as revoking compromised <b>IAM</b> keys or updating <b>WAF</b> rules to block an attacker's IP." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="370" x="1230" y="1000" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="RduPqYsKie_LVsf-Kyjq-16" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;fontSize=14;fontFamily=Tahoma;" value="<div class="container"><div class="markdown markdown-main-panel stronger enable-updated-hr-color" id="model-response-message-contentr_8f339d23bf738bf5" aria-live="polite" aria-busy="false" dir="ltr" style="--animation-duration: 400ms; --fade-animation-function: ease-out;"><p data-path-to-node="0">This project is a security-first, modern web application architecture built entirely on AWS using serverless components. User traffic is protected and optimized at the edge via <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="177">AWS WAF</b> and <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="189">CloudFront</b>, while authentication is securely managed through <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="250">AWS Cognito</b>. On the backend, <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="279">Lambda</b> functions operate under the principle of <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="327">Least Privilege</b>, storing data in <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="360">KMS-encrypted DynamoDB</b> and <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="387">S3</b>. The most advanced feature of the system is its proactive defense layer, which utilizes <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="478">GuardDuty</b> and <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="492">EventBridge</b> to trigger an <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="518">Automated Remediation Lambda</b> for instant threat response. Overall, this architecture represents a comprehensive <b data-path-to-node="0" data-index-in-node="630">DevSecOps</b> model that balances high scalability and cost-efficiency with enterprise-grade security.</p></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="110" width="950" x="169.91" y="985" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

div.h1: Zero Trust Serverless Application with Threat Detection

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 85px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 85px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
The request first hits the Web Application Firewall.
It inspects the traffic for common exploits like SQL Injection or Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and applies rate-limiting to block bots.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 155px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 155px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: If the request is safe, it reaches the CDN. If does'nt WAF block the user.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 225px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 235px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: CloudFront fetches your static assets (React/Vue/HTML) from an S3 bucket (secured via OAC) and serves them to the user from the nearest edge location.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 315px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 315px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
The user logs in. Their credentials go directly to Cognito.
Upon successful authentication, Cognito issues a JWT (JSON Web Token). This token is stored int browser and attached to the header of every subsequent API requests.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 403px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 413px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p: When the user performs an action (e.g., POST /create-todo), the request hits the API Gateway. The Gateway automatically validates the JWT. If the token is missing or invalid, the request is rejected immediately at the door, saving you from unnecessary Lambda execution costs.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 504px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 504px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p: Once authorized, the request triggers a Lambda function.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 585px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 58px; height: 1px; padding-top: 300px; margin-left: 516px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 585px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Instead of hardcoding credentials, Lambda retrieves them from Secrets Manager. The diagram also highlights Tokenization Logic to protect sensitive data during processing.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 665px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: All data at rest in DynamoDB and S3 is encrypted using AWS KMS keys to ensure compliance and data security.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 38px; height: 1px; padding-top: 745px; margin-left: 1186px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

div.font: CloudTrail logs every API call, while GuardDuty uses machine learning to analyze those logs for malicious activity or unauthorized

div.div: When GuardDuty finds a threat, it triggers an event in Amazon EventBridge.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 910px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: EventBridge sends a real-time alert via SNS to notify the security team via email or SMS.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 995px; margin-left: 1237px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: This is the most advanced part of the flow. A dedicated Lambda function is automatically triggered to take immediate action, such as revoking compromised IAM keys or updating WAF rules to block an attacker's IP.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 948px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1005px; margin-left: 177px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Tahoma; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
--animation-duration: 400ms; --fade-animation-function: ease-out;
div.p: This project is a security-first, modern web application architecture built entirely on AWS using serverless components. User traffic is protected and optimized at the edge via AWS WAF and CloudFront, while authentication is securely managed through AWS Cognito. On the backend, Lambda functions operate under the principle of Least Privilege, storing data in KMS-encrypted DynamoDB and S3. The most advanced feature of the system is its proactive defense layer, which utilizes GuardDuty and EventBridge to trigger an Automated Remediation Lambda for instant threat response. Overall, this architecture represents a comprehensive DevSecOps model that balances high scalability and cost-efficiency with enterprise-grade security.
